pair.missing_credentials: Please enter your Google OAuth Client ID and Client Secret in the app settings first (More > Apps > Google Health > Configure app).
pair.not_authorized: Google login was not completed. Please try again.

device.auth_lost: Google login expired. Open the device and use 'Repair' to log in again.
device.missing_scopes: No Google permission for: __types__. Use 'Repair' and tick ALL checkboxes on the Google consent screen.

settings.title: Google Health
settings.intro: This app needs its own Google Cloud OAuth client, because Google grants Health API access per project.
settings.step1: Open the Google Cloud Console and create (or select) a project.
settings.step2: Enable the Google Health API for the project.
settings.step3: Create an OAuth client ID of type 'Web application' and add this authorized redirect URI:
settings.step4: While the OAuth consent screen is in 'Testing' mode, add your own Google account as a test user. Note: in testing mode Google expires logins after 7 days — publish the consent screen for permanent access.
settings.step5: Paste the Client ID and Client Secret below, save, then add the device. Important: on the Google sign-in screen, tick ALL permission checkboxes — Google leaves them unchecked by default.
settings.enable_write: Allow writing to Google Health (needed for the 'Log weight' Flow card)
settings.enable_write_hint: Changing this only affects devices paired afterwards. Re-pair existing devices to apply.
